speaker: EDMUND
text: Some officers take them away. Good guard

speaker: EDMUND
text: Until their greater pleasures first be known

speaker: EDMUND
text: That are to censure them.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: We are not the first

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Who with best meaning have incurred the worst.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: For thee, oppressèd king, I am cast down.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Myself could else outfrown false Fortune’s frown.

speaker: CORDELIA
text: Shall we not see these daughters and these sisters?

speaker: LEAR
text: No, no, no, no. Come, let’s away to prison.

speaker: LEAR
text: We two alone will sing like birds i’ th’ cage.

speaker: LEAR
text: When thou dost ask me blessing, I’ll kneel down

speaker: LEAR
text: And ask of thee forgiveness. So we’ll live,

speaker: LEAR
text: And pray, and sing, and tell old tales, and laugh

speaker: LEAR
text: At gilded butterflies, and hear poor rogues

speaker: LEAR
text: Talk of court news, and we’ll talk with them too—

speaker: LEAR
text: Who loses and who wins; who’s in, who’s out—

speaker: LEAR
text: And take upon ’s the mystery of things,

speaker: LEAR
text: As if we were God’s spies. And we’ll wear out,

speaker: LEAR
text: In a walled prison, packs and sects of great ones

speaker: LEAR
text: That ebb and flow by th’ moon.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Take them away.

speaker: LEAR
text: Upon such sacrifices, my Cordelia,

speaker: LEAR
text: The gods themselves throw incense. Have I caught thee?

speaker: LEAR
text: He that parts us shall bring a brand from heaven

speaker: LEAR
text: And fire us hence like foxes. Wipe thine eyes.

speaker: LEAR
text: The good years shall devour them, flesh and fell,

speaker: LEAR
text: Ere they shall make us weep. We’ll see ’em starved first.

speaker: LEAR
text: Come.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Come hither, captain. Hark.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Take thou this note. Go follow them to prison.

speaker: EDMUND
text: One step I have advanced thee. If thou dost

speaker: EDMUND
text: As this instructs thee, thou dost make thy way

speaker: EDMUND
text: To noble fortunes. Know thou this: that men

speaker: EDMUND
text: Are as the time is; to be tender-minded

speaker: EDMUND
text: Does not become a sword. Thy great employment

speaker: EDMUND
text: Will not bear question. Either say thou ’lt do ’t,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Or thrive by other means.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I’ll do ’t, my lord.

speaker: EDMUND
text: About it, and write “happy” when th’ hast done.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Mark, I say, instantly, and carry it so

speaker: EDMUND
text: As I have set it down.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I cannot draw a cart, nor eat dried oats.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: If it be man’s work, I’ll do ’t.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Sir, you have showed today your valiant strain,

speaker: ALBANY
text: And Fortune led you well. You have the captives

speaker: ALBANY
text: Who were the opposites of this day’s strife.

speaker: ALBANY
text: I do require them of you, so to use them

speaker: ALBANY
text: As we shall find their merits and our safety

speaker: ALBANY
text: May equally determine.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Sir, I thought it fit

speaker: EDMUND
text: To send the old and miserable king

speaker: EDMUND
text: To some retention and appointed guard,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Whose age had charms in it, whose title more,

speaker: EDMUND
text: To pluck the common bosom on his side

speaker: EDMUND
text: And turn our impressed lances in our eyes,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Which do command them. With him I sent the Queen,

speaker: EDMUND
text: My reason all the same, and they are ready

speaker: EDMUND
text: Tomorrow, or at further space, t’ appear

speaker: EDMUND
text: Where you shall hold your session. At this time

speaker: EDMUND
text: We sweat and bleed. The friend hath lost his friend,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And the best quarrels in the heat are cursed

speaker: EDMUND
text: By those that feel their sharpness.

speaker: EDMUND
text: The question of Cordelia and her father

speaker: EDMUND
text: Requires a fitter place.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Sir, by your patience,

speaker: ALBANY
text: I hold you but a subject of this war,

speaker: ALBANY
text: Not as a brother.

speaker: REGAN
text: That’s as we list to grace him.

speaker: REGAN
text: Methinks our pleasure might have been demanded

speaker: REGAN
text: Ere you had spoke so far. He led our powers,

speaker: REGAN
text: Bore the commission of my place and person,

speaker: REGAN
text: The which immediacy may well stand up

speaker: REGAN
text: And call itself your brother.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Not so hot.

speaker: GONERIL
text: In his own grace he doth exalt himself

speaker: GONERIL
text: More than in your addition.

speaker: REGAN
text: In my rights,

speaker: REGAN
text: By me invested, he compeers the best.

speaker: GONERIL
text: That were the most if he should husband you.

speaker: REGAN
text: Jesters do oft prove prophets.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Holla, holla!

speaker: GONERIL
text: That eye that told you so looked but asquint.

speaker: REGAN
text: Lady, I am not well, else I should answer

speaker: REGAN
text: From a full-flowing stomach.

speaker: REGAN
text: General,

speaker: REGAN
text: Take thou my soldiers, prisoners, patrimony.

speaker: REGAN
text: Dispose of them, of me; the walls is thine.

speaker: REGAN
text: Witness the world that I create thee here

speaker: REGAN
text: My lord and master.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Mean you to enjoy him?

speaker: ALBANY
text: The let-alone lies not in your goodwill.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Nor in thine, lord.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Half-blooded fellow, yes.

speaker: REGAN
text: Let the drum strike, and prove my title thine.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Stay yet, hear reason.—Edmund, I arrest thee

speaker: ALBANY
text: On capital treason; and, in thine attaint,

speaker: ALBANY
text: This gilded serpent.—For your claim, fair sister,

speaker: ALBANY
text: I bar it in the interest of my wife.

speaker: ALBANY
text: ’Tis she is subcontracted to this lord,

speaker: ALBANY
text: And I, her husband, contradict your banns.

speaker: ALBANY
text: If you will marry, make your loves to me.

speaker: ALBANY
text: My lady is bespoke.

speaker: GONERIL
text: An interlude!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Thou art armed, Gloucester. Let the trumpet sound.

speaker: ALBANY
text: If none appear to prove upon thy person

speaker: ALBANY
text: Thy heinous, manifest, and many treasons,

speaker: ALBANY
text: There is my pledge.

speaker: ALBANY
text: I’ll make it on thy heart,

speaker: ALBANY
text: Ere I taste bread, thou art in nothing less

speaker: ALBANY
text: Than I have here proclaimed thee.

speaker: REGAN
text: Sick, O, sick!

speaker: GONERIL
text: If not, I’ll ne’er trust medicine.

speaker: EDMUND
text: There’s my exchange.

speaker: EDMUND
text: What in the world he is

speaker: EDMUND
text: That names me traitor, villain-like he lies.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Call by the trumpet. He that dares approach,

speaker: EDMUND
text: On him, on you, who not, I will maintain

speaker: EDMUND
text: My truth and honor firmly.

speaker: ALBANY
text: A herald, ho!

speaker: EDMUND
text: A herald, ho, a herald!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Trust to thy single virtue, for thy soldiers,

speaker: ALBANY
text: All levied in my name, have in my name

speaker: ALBANY
text: Took their discharge.

speaker: REGAN
text: My sickness grows upon me.

speaker: ALBANY
text: She is not well. Convey her to my tent.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Come hither, herald. Let the trumpet sound,

speaker: ALBANY
text: And read out this.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Sound, trumpet!

speaker: HERALD
text:
reads

              If
               
              any
               
              man
               
              of
               
              quality
               
              or

              ,

              of

              ,
               
              will
               
              maintain
               
              upon
               
              Edmund
              ,

              Earl
               
              of

              ,
               
              that
               
              he
               
              is
               
              a

              traitor
              ,
               
              let
               
              him
              
              appear
               
              by
               
              the

              sound
               
              of
               
              the

              He
               
              is
               
              bold
               
              in
              
              his
               
              defense

speaker: HERALD
text: Again!

speaker: HERALD
text: Again!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Ask him his purposes, why he appears

speaker: ALBANY
text: Upon this call o’ th’ trumpet.

speaker: HERALD
text: What are you?

speaker: HERALD
text: Your name, your quality, and why you answer

speaker: HERALD
text: This present summons?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Know my name is lost,

speaker: EDGAR
text: By treason’s tooth bare-gnawn and canker-bit.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Yet am I noble as the adversary

speaker: EDGAR
text: I come to cope.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Which is that adversary?

speaker: EDGAR
text: What’s he that speaks for Edmund, Earl of Gloucester?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Himself. What sayest thou to him?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Draw thy sword,

speaker: EDGAR
text: That if my speech offend a noble heart,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thy arm may do thee justice. Here is mine.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Behold, it is my privilege, the privilege of mine honors,

speaker: EDGAR
text: My oath, and my profession. I protest,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Maugre thy strength, place, youth, and eminence,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Despite thy victor-sword and fire-new fortune,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thy valor, and thy heart, thou art a traitor,

speaker: EDGAR
text: False to thy gods, thy brother, and thy father,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Conspirant ’gainst this high illustrious prince,

speaker: EDGAR
text: And from th’ extremest upward of thy head

speaker: EDGAR
text: To the descent and dust below thy foot,

speaker: EDGAR
text: A most toad-spotted traitor. Say thou “no,”

speaker: EDGAR
text: This sword, this arm, and my best spirits are bent

speaker: EDGAR
text: To prove upon thy heart, whereto I speak,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thou liest.

speaker: EDMUND
text: In wisdom I should ask thy name,

speaker: EDMUND
text: But since thy outside looks so fair and warlike,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And that thy tongue some say of breeding breathes,

speaker: EDMUND
text: What safe and nicely I might well delay

speaker: EDMUND
text: By rule of knighthood, I disdain and spurn.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Back do I toss these treasons to thy head,

speaker: EDMUND
text: With the hell-hated lie o’erwhelm thy heart,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Which, for they yet glance by and scarcely bruise,

speaker: EDMUND
text: This sword of mine shall give them instant way,

speaker: EDMUND
text: Where they shall rest forever. Trumpets, speak!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Save him, save him!

speaker: GONERIL
text: This is practice, Gloucester.

speaker: GONERIL
text: By th’ law of war, thou wast not bound to answer

speaker: GONERIL
text: An unknown opposite. Thou art not vanquished,

speaker: GONERIL
text: But cozened and beguiled.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Shut your mouth, dame,

speaker: ALBANY
text: Or with this paper shall I stopple it.—Hold, sir.—

speaker: ALBANY
text: Thou worse than any name, read thine own evil.

speaker: ALBANY
text: No tearing, lady. I perceive you know it.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Say if I do; the laws are mine, not thine.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Who can arraign me for ’t?

speaker: ALBANY
text: Most monstrous! O!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Know’st thou this paper?

speaker: GONERIL
text: Ask me not what I know.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Go after her, she’s desperate. Govern her.

speaker: EDMUND
text: What you have charged me with, that have I done,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And more, much more. The time will bring it out.

speaker: EDMUND
text: ’Tis past, and so am I. But what art thou

speaker: EDMUND
text: That hast this fortune on me? If thou ’rt noble,

speaker: EDMUND
text: I do forgive thee.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Let’s exchange charity.

speaker: EDGAR
text: I am no less in blood than thou art, Edmund;

speaker: EDGAR
text: If more, the more th’ hast wronged me.

speaker: EDGAR
text: My name is Edgar and thy father’s son.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The gods are just, and of our pleasant vices

speaker: EDGAR
text: Make instruments to plague us.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The dark and vicious place where thee he got

speaker: EDGAR
text: Cost him his eyes.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Th’ hast spoken right. ’Tis true.

speaker: EDMUND
text: The wheel is come full circle; I am here.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Methought thy very gait did prophesy

speaker: ALBANY
text: A royal nobleness. I must embrace thee.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Let sorrow split my heart if ever I

speaker: ALBANY
text: Did hate thee or thy father!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Worthy prince, I know ’t.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Where have you hid yourself?

speaker: ALBANY
text: How have you known the miseries of your father?

speaker: EDGAR
text: By nursing them, my lord. List a brief tale,

speaker: EDGAR
text: And when ’tis told, O, that my heart would burst!

speaker: EDGAR
text: The bloody proclamation to escape

speaker: EDGAR
text: That followed me so near—O, our lives’ sweetness,

speaker: EDGAR
text: That we the pain of death would hourly die

speaker: EDGAR
text: Rather than die at once!—taught me to shift

speaker: EDGAR
text: Into a madman’s rags, t’ assume a semblance

speaker: EDGAR
text: That very dogs disdained, and in this habit

speaker: EDGAR
text: Met I my father with his bleeding rings,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Their precious stones new lost; became his guide,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Led him, begged for him, saved him from despair.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Never—O fault!—revealed myself unto him

speaker: EDGAR
text: Until some half hour past, when I was armed.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Not sure, though hoping of this good success,

speaker: EDGAR
text: I asked his blessing, and from first to last

speaker: EDGAR
text: Told him our pilgrimage. But his flawed heart

speaker: EDGAR
text: (Alack, too weak the conflict to support)

speaker: EDGAR
text: ’Twixt two extremes of passion, joy and grief,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Burst smilingly.

speaker: EDMUND
text: This speech of yours hath moved me,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And shall perchance do good. But speak you on.

speaker: EDMUND
text: You look as you had something more to say.

speaker: ALBANY
text: If there be more, more woeful, hold it in,

speaker: ALBANY
text: For I am almost ready to dissolve,

speaker: ALBANY
text: Hearing of this.

speaker: EDGAR
text: This would have seemed a period

speaker: EDGAR
text: To such as love not sorrow; but another,

speaker: EDGAR
text: To amplify too much, would make much more

speaker: EDGAR
text: And top extremity. Whilst I

speaker: EDGAR
text: Was big in clamor, came there in a man

speaker: EDGAR
text: Who, having seen me in my worst estate,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Shunned my abhorred society; but then, finding

speaker: EDGAR
text: Who ’twas that so endured, with his strong arms

speaker: EDGAR
text: He fastened on my neck and bellowed out

speaker: EDGAR
text: As he’d burst heaven, threw him on my father,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Told the most piteous tale of Lear and him

speaker: EDGAR
text: That ever ear received, which, in recounting,

speaker: EDGAR
text: His grief grew puissant, and the strings of life

speaker: EDGAR
text: Began to crack. Twice then the trumpets sounded,

speaker: EDGAR
text: And there I left him tranced.

speaker: ALBANY
text: But who was this?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Kent, sir, the banished Kent, who in disguise

speaker: EDGAR
text: Followed his enemy king and did him service

speaker: EDGAR
text: Improper for a slave.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Help, help, O, help!

speaker: EDGAR
text: What kind of help?

speaker: ALBANY
text: Speak, man!

speaker: EDGAR
text: What means this bloody knife?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis hot, it smokes! It came even from the heart

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Of—O, she’s dead!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Who dead? Speak, man.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Your lady, sir, your lady. And her sister

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: By her is poisoned. She confesses it.

speaker: EDMUND
text: I was contracted to them both. All three

speaker: EDMUND
text: Now marry in an instant.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Here comes Kent.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Produce the bodies, be they alive or dead.

speaker: ALBANY
text: This judgment of the heavens, that makes us tremble,

speaker: ALBANY
text: Touches us not with pity. O, is this he?

speaker: ALBANY
text:
To
                 
                Kent
                .
                 
              The time will not allow the compliment

speaker: ALBANY
text: Which very manners urges.

speaker: KENT
text: I am come

speaker: KENT
text: To bid my king and master aye goodnight.

speaker: KENT
text: Is he not here?

speaker: ALBANY
text: Great thing of us forgot!

speaker: ALBANY
text: Speak, Edmund, where’s the King? And where’s Cordelia?

speaker: ALBANY
text: Seest thou this object, Kent?

speaker: KENT
text: Alack, why thus?

speaker: EDMUND
text: Yet Edmund was beloved.

speaker: EDMUND
text: The one the other poisoned for my sake,

speaker: EDMUND
text: And after slew herself.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Even so.—Cover their faces.

speaker: EDMUND
text: I pant for life. Some good I mean to do

speaker: EDMUND
text: Despite of mine own nature. Quickly send—

speaker: EDMUND
text: Be brief in it—to th’ castle, for my writ

speaker: EDMUND
text: Is on the life of Lear, and on Cordelia.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Nay, send in time.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Run, run, O, run!

speaker: EDGAR
text:
To who, my lord?
                 
                To
                 
                Edmund
                .
               Who has the office? Send

speaker: EDGAR
text: Thy token of reprieve.

speaker: EDMUND
text: Well thought on. Take my sword. Give it the Captain.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Haste thee for thy life.

speaker: EDMUND
text: He hath commission from thy wife and me

speaker: EDMUND
text: To hang Cordelia in the prison, and

speaker: EDMUND
text: To lay the blame upon her own despair,

speaker: EDMUND
text: That she fordid herself.

speaker: ALBANY
text: The gods defend her!—Bear him hence awhile.

speaker: LEAR
text: Howl, howl, howl! O, you are men of stones!

speaker: LEAR
text: Had I your tongues and eyes, I’d use them so

speaker: LEAR
text: That heaven’s vault should crack. She’s gone forever.

speaker: LEAR
text: I know when one is dead and when one lives.

speaker: LEAR
text: She’s dead as earth.—Lend me a looking glass.

speaker: LEAR
text: If that her breath will mist or stain the stone,

speaker: LEAR
text: Why, then she lives.

speaker: KENT
text: Is this the promised end?

speaker: EDGAR
text: Or image of that horror?

speaker: ALBANY
text: Fall and cease.

speaker: LEAR
text: This feather stirs. She lives. If it be so,

speaker: LEAR
text: It is a chance which does redeem all sorrows

speaker: LEAR
text: That ever I have felt.

speaker: KENT
text: O, my good master—

speaker: LEAR
text: Prithee, away.

speaker: EDGAR
text: ’Tis noble Kent, your friend.

speaker: LEAR
text: A plague upon you, murderers, traitors all!

speaker: LEAR
text: I might have saved her. Now she’s gone forever.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Cordelia, Cordelia, stay a little. Ha!

speaker: LEAR
text: What is ’t thou sayst?—Her voice was ever soft,

speaker: LEAR
text: Gentle, and low, an excellent thing in woman.

speaker: LEAR
text: I killed the slave that was a-hanging thee.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: ’Tis true, my lords, he did.

speaker: LEAR
text: Did I not, fellow?

speaker: LEAR
text: I have seen the day, with my good biting falchion

speaker: LEAR
text: I would have made him skip. I am old now,

speaker: LEAR
text:
And these same crosses spoil me.
                 
                To
                 
                Kent
                .
               Who are you?

speaker: LEAR
text: Mine eyes are not o’ th’ best. I’ll tell you straight.

speaker: KENT
text: If Fortune brag of two she loved and hated,

speaker: KENT
text: One of them we behold.

speaker: LEAR
text: This is a dull sight. Are you not Kent?

speaker: KENT
text: The same,

speaker: KENT
text: Your servant Kent. Where is your servant Caius?

speaker: LEAR
text: He’s a good fellow, I can tell you that.

speaker: LEAR
text: He’ll strike and quickly too. He’s dead and rotten.

speaker: KENT
text: No, my good lord, I am the very man—

speaker: LEAR
text: I’ll see that straight.

speaker: KENT
text: That from your first of difference and decay

speaker: KENT
text: Have followed your sad steps.

speaker: LEAR
text: You are welcome hither.

speaker: KENT
text: Nor no man else. All’s cheerless, dark, and deadly.

speaker: KENT
text: Your eldest daughters have fordone themselves,

speaker: KENT
text: And desperately are dead.

speaker: LEAR
text: Ay, so I think.

speaker: ALBANY
text: He knows not what he says, and vain is it

speaker: ALBANY
text: That we present us to him.

speaker: EDGAR
text: Very bootless.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Edmund is dead, my lord.

speaker: ALBANY
text: That’s but a trifle here.—

speaker: ALBANY
text: You lords and noble friends, know our intent:

speaker: ALBANY
text: What comfort to this great decay may come

speaker: ALBANY
text: Shall be applied. For us, we will resign,

speaker: ALBANY
text: During the life of this old Majesty,

speaker: ALBANY
text: To him our absolute power; you to your rights,

speaker: ALBANY
text: With boot and such addition as your Honors

speaker: ALBANY
text: Have more than merited. All friends shall taste

speaker: ALBANY
text: The wages of their virtue, and all foes

speaker: ALBANY
text: The cup of their deservings. O, see, see!

speaker: LEAR
text: And my poor fool is hanged. No, no, no life?

speaker: LEAR
text: Why should a dog, a horse, a rat have life,

speaker: LEAR
text: And thou no breath at all? Thou ’lt come no more,

speaker: LEAR
text: Never, never, never, never, never.—

speaker: LEAR
text: Pray you undo this button. Thank you, sir.

speaker: LEAR
text: Do you see this? Look on her, look, her lips,

speaker: LEAR
text: Look there, look there!

speaker: EDGAR
text:
He faints.
                 
                To
                 
                Lear
                .
               My lord, my lord!

speaker: KENT
text: Break, heart, I prithee, break!

speaker: EDGAR
text: Look up, my lord.

speaker: KENT
text: Vex not his ghost. O, let him pass! He hates him

speaker: KENT
text: That would upon the rack of this tough world

speaker: KENT
text: Stretch him out longer.

speaker: EDGAR
text: He is gone indeed.

speaker: KENT
text: The wonder is he hath endured so long.

speaker: KENT
text: He but usurped his life.

speaker: ALBANY
text: Bear them from hence. Our present business

speaker: ALBANY
text:
Is general woe.
                 
                To
                 
                Edgar
                 
                and
                 
                Kent
                .
               Friends of my soul, you twain

speaker: ALBANY
text: Rule in this realm, and the gored state sustain.

speaker: KENT
text: I have a journey, sir, shortly to go;

speaker: KENT
text: My master calls me. I must not say no.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The weight of this sad time we must obey,

speaker: EDGAR
text: Speak what we feel, not what we ought to say.

speaker: EDGAR
text: The oldest hath borne most; we that are young

speaker: EDGAR
text: Shall never see so much nor live so long.
